title: chamaecyparis thyoides var. henryae
intro: Chamaecyparis thyoides var. henryae, also known as Atlantic White Cypress or Henry's False Cypress, is a variety of coniferous tree that belongs to the cypress family. Native to the southeastern United States, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chamaecyparis thyoides var. henryae include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and twigs of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential interactions with other medications.
